steps.title: Group matching bases
steps.content:
When multiplying powers with the same base, add the exponents. First group the factors by base.
$$(m^4q^3z^{-1})(mq^6z^3)=(m^4\cdot m^1)(q^3\cdot q^6)(z^{-1}\cdot z^3)$$

steps.title: Add the exponents
steps.content:
Apply the product rule to each variable separately. The exponent on $m$ is $4+1=5$, the exponent on $q$ is $3+6=9$, and the exponent on $z$ is $-1+3=2$.
$$m^{4+1}q^{3+6}z^{-1+3}=m^5q^9z^2$$
The simplified expression is ${m}^{5}{q}^{9}{z}^{2}$. The answer is B.
